text.title: nosql-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for nosql-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Embed child documents when they are always accessed with the parent and the child set is bounded (1:few); reference when the relationship is 1:many or many:many.
rule.rationale: Embedding avoids joins and keeps reads in one round-trip; unbounded embedded arrays grow the document past the 16MB limit and degrade index performance.

rule.statement: Use the bucket pattern for time-series data: group N measurements per document with a summary sub-document; set a TTL index for automatic expiration.
rule.rationale: One document per measurement creates millions of tiny documents, degrading index performance. Buckets reduce document count by 100-1000x.

rule.statement: Enable $jsonSchema validation on production MongoDB collections; loose schemas rot and allow bad data to accumulate.
rule.rationale: Schema validation catches incompatible documents at write-time before they corrupt downstream queries and reports.

rule.statement: Pick Redis data structures by access pattern: String for simple cache; Hash for object fields; List for queues; Set for membership; Sorted Set for leaderboards/rate-limiting; Stream for event sourcing.
rule.rationale: Using the wrong structure forces workarounds (e.g., serializing JSON into a String when Hash would allow field-level updates) and degrades performance.

rule.statement: Set TTL on every cache and session key; never store data in Redis without an expiry or explicit eviction strategy.
rule.rationale: Keys without TTL accumulate until Redis runs out of memory; the server then evicts randomly or stops accepting writes.

rule.statement: Prefix all keys with {service}:{entity}:{id} to prevent collisions across services sharing one Redis instance.
rule.rationale: Agents generate raw keys (user:123) that collide across services; namespacing avoids cross-service data corruption.

rule.statement: Use Redis Streams (XADD/XREADGROUP) instead of Pub/Sub when message durability or consumer groups are required; Pub/Sub is fire-and-forget with no replay.
rule.rationale: Pub/Sub messages are lost if no subscriber is connected; Streams persist messages and support consumer groups with acknowledgement.

rule.statement: Design one table per access pattern in Cassandra; partition key selection determines scalability and cannot be changed without a full table copy.
rule.rationale: Cassandra has no JOINs and no secondary indexes without performance penalty; the partition key must match the primary query pattern exactly.

rule.statement: Use (entity_id, time_bucket) as the partition key for time-series data to prevent hot partitions on single-entity keys under high write load.
rule.rationale: A single partition key (sensor_id alone) concentrates all writes to one node; time-bucketing distributes writes across nodes.

rule.statement: Index every node property used in MATCH predicates; missing indexes cause full graph scans.
rule.rationale: Neo4j traversal is fast, but initial node lookup without an index requires scanning all nodes of a label.
